mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts show a mean return <= 0.0% with p-value > 0.10 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing the 4.0% threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports short-term earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during early-cycle expansions yields a mean 5-day forward return <= 0.0% or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index year-over-year growth rate exceeding 2.5% triggers inflation expectations and anticipated central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in > 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic activity and rising consumer spending power, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of instances, or the series exhibits a Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and month-over-month growth in retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro and micro indicators, reinforcing bullish equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a classification accuracy > 55%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising consumer prices indicates a stagflationary supply shock, which erodes profit margins and triggers risk-off positioning, driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Stagflationary divergence (INDPRO down, CPIAUCSL up) is followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in historical stagflation periods.
